title: The Importance of a Balanced Diet
passage:
A Balanced Diet for Healthy Living

A balanced diet is essential for maintaining good health and well-being. It provides the necessary nutrients that your body needs to function correctly. A balanced diet includes a variety of foods: fruits, vegetables, proteins, carbohydrates, and fats. Each food group provides different nutrients that work together to keep the body healthy.

Fruits and vegetables are rich in vitamins, minerals, and fiber. They help reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and diabetes. It is recommended to eat at least five portions of fruits and vegetables every day. Proteins, which can be found in meat, fish, eggs, and beans, are crucial for growth and repair of body tissues.

Carbohydrates are the body's main source of energy. Foods such as bread, rice, and pasta provide the energy needed for daily activities. However, it is important to choose whole grains, which offer more nutrients and fiber than refined grains. Fats, while often viewed negatively, are also important for health. Healthy fats, found in foods like avocados, nuts, and olive oil, support cell growth and protect organs.

To maintain a balanced diet, it is important to be mindful of portion sizes and to limit the intake of sugar and salt. Reducing processed foods can also greatly improve dietary quality. Drinking plenty of water and staying active are vital components that complement a balanced diet, leading to improved health outcomes and a better quality of life.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What is the main purpose of the passage?

options.A: To explain the benefits of a balanced diet.
options.B: To list different food groups and their nutrients.
options.C: To discourage the intake of carbohydrates.
options.D: To promote processed foods for better health.

questions.explanation: The passage emphasizes the importance of a balanced diet for maintaining good health and outlines its benefits.

questions.instruction: Decide if the following statements are True, False, or Not Given.
questions.text: Eating five portions of fruits and vegetables daily can help prevent heart disease.
questions.explanation: The passage states that fruits and vegetables help reduce the risk of chronic diseases, including heart disease.

questions.instruction: Answer Yes, No or Not Given.
questions.text: The author believes that all fats are unhealthy.
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that healthy fats are important for health, indicating the author's belief that not all fats are unhealthy.

questions.instruction: Match the headings to the paragraphs.

text.A: Nutritional Benefits of Various Food Groups

questions.explanation: The headings correspond to the main ideas of each paragraph in the passage.

questions.instruction: Complete the sentence with NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS.
questions.text: Fruits and vegetables are important for reducing the risk of _____ diseases.
questions.explanation: The passage specifically mentions that fruits and vegetables help reduce the risk of chronic diseases.

vocabulary.meaning: Substances in food that provide nourishment essential for the maintenance of life.
A term used to describe a condition that is long-lasting and persistent.
Foods that have been altered from their natural state for safety reasons or convenience.

tactics:
Hướng dẫn

When reading the passage, focus on the main ideas of each paragraph and how they relate to the overall theme of a balanced diet. Pay attention to key terms that indicate the benefits of different food groups. Remember to check for details that support the statements in the questions.
